title: widget
text: widget An element in a user interface that represents an entity, field, or operation call. It determines the appearance and behavior of the field, and the way the user enters or selects values in the field. Do not use UDO widgets. Determines the appearance and behavior of a field. In character mode, a widget that is not supported is mapped to another widget type. Click a widget to give the field focus. Do not use "click on". A field is painted on a component; the widget type is a property of the field. Each widget type has properties of its own. A field has a value; a widget does not. A physical widget can be "standard" or "custom". A physical widget can be mapped to one or more logical widgets in the initialization file. (The logical widget name appears in the Widget Type list on the Define Field form.) Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
